rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations that reference component ports
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
generic_rules: Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Place guarantees in the appropriate scope based on whether they relate to component type or implementation features
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assign` only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Omit clauses involving event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons unless the model provides an explicit data value
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
